Crime tops CAR agenda for '94

Gwen Carleton NICAR December 1994
Crime & JusticeInvestigative Methods

The year's CAR reporting saw a significant focus on crime, reflecting national legislative interest with initiatives like the Brady Bill. Noteworthy projects included the Wisconsin State Journal's 'Ci...

The year's CAR reporting saw a significant focus on crime, reflecting national legislative interest with initiatives like the Brady Bill. Noteworthy projects included the Wisconsin State Journal's 'City of Hope' series that examined the correlation between crime and socio-economic factors in Madison. In Southern California, serious study into juvenile crime patterns revealed a shift towards more severe crimes among teens.

USA Today's special report highlighted disparities in prosecutor effectiveness across different cities, uncovering a selective enforcement pattern driven by resource allocation and federal law avalanches. These stories highlight just a portion of the investigative climate fostered by CAR efforts in 1994.
